test.note: All 219 tests pass. Tests added for procedure delete audit events (5 tests) and audit query API (24 tests).

typecheck.note: Both web and server typecheck pass

blockingIssuesFromPriorRound.issue: Procedure delete/remove operation does not create any audit event (VAL-AUDIT-012)
blockingIssuesFromPriorRound.resolution: Both soft delete (when service requests exist) and hard delete (when no service requests exist) now create audit_event entries with action='delete'. 5 tests added.

blockingIssuesFromPriorRound.issue: ipAddress and userAgent fields are never populated in audit events (VAL-AUDIT-010)
blockingIssuesFromPriorRound.resolution: extractClientInfo() helper captures IP (x-forwarded-for or direct) and user-agent from headers. All mutation routers now pass clientInfo to createAuditEvent: bookings, citizen, procedures, reservation-series, reservations, staff, schedule routers.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Clarify that 'update' documentation rule in governance section applies to ALL mutations including delete/remove operations. The prior audit-event-instrumentation handoff claimed complete coverage but missed delete paths.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: audit-event-instrumentation handoff said 'Added audit events to procedure CRUD' but delete/remove was missing. VAL-AUDIT-012 specifically requires procedure delete to be logged.

assertionsCovered.VAL-AUDIT-010: FIXED - ipAddress and userAgent now populated via extractClientInfo()
assertionsCovered.VAL-AUDIT-012: FIXED - procedure delete now creates audit events
